data.description:
FOLLOW-UP (surfaced by the prior-art validation, PA1). Evaluate migrating the langgraph runner's structured-output from explicit `ToolStrategy(handle_errors=True)` to raw-schema/**AutoStrategy→ProviderStrategy** (provider-native, decode-time schema enforcement). This is the current LangChain-recommended idiom and would make the BI-1 optional-None-leakage class of bug structurally impossible on supporting providers.

## Context
The completion-verifier feature keeps ToolStrategy (provider-portable, tested). The original "disable extended thinking because forced tool-choice conflicts" rationale was fixed upstream (LangChain PR #35544, Mar 2026). Migration is **framework-wide** (affects review/code/scan + the completion verifier) and needs its own eval pass — hence a separate ticket.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spike: AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy behavior + provider matrix (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/local) vs current ToolStrategy
- [ ] Decide keep-ToolStrategy vs migrate; if migrate, structured-output behavior unchanged for review/code/scan (evals green)
- [ ] Remove the stale "disable thinking" rationale from runner docs regardless of decision
data.title: Follow-up: evaluate AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy structured-output (framework-wide)
